author: garyrong
content: True. But we already did it even before the Amsterdam, We refund `params.CallNewAccountGas - params.TxAuthTupleGas)` before also.

author: misilva73
content:
> I think one thing I disagree is: we can't use the one rule in mempool validation while another rule in block processing. If we need to enforce this property with a consistent precondition, then the validation must be stateless.
I agree with this and this was my main concern

author: misilva73
content: On this, we were trying to not change how execution refunds work today. We did change how state creation refunds work, and that is already a big change. I would rather think about execution gas refunds in a more holistic way in a dedicated eip

author: misilva73
content: I think we are agreeing that we want to have a resource-based refund for auths and the discussino has mostly been on how to do it. I still find it strange to change the intrinsic costs, even though it seems easier to explain. I would rather do it to gas_left and reservoir using the same LIFO approach

author: misilva73
content: would that be ok for you, <@1252939266871263294> ? Should I update the EIP in a new PR or do you prefer to update your PR instead?
